summary: Cathy O'Neil celebrates the press coverage received for 'Occupy Finance,' a book released by the Occupy Wall Street Alternative Banking group on the movement's second anniversary. Despite discovering a missing page after publication, the book garnered attention from major outlets including NPR, Bloomberg, the New York Times, and the Financial Times. The post serves as both a celebration of the book's media reception and a practical announcement about how readers can obtain copies.
key_points: Occupy Finance received coverage from major financial and news outlets including FT Alphaville, NPR, Bloomberg, and the New York Times
The book launch coincided with the second anniversary of the Occupy movement
A production error left a page missing, which was quickly made available online as a PDF
The grassroots nature of the project is evident in the envelope-stuffing distribution method
money_quotes: Yesterday was pretty amazing, in spite of the fact that we realized a page was missing from the book.
Our group meeting this Sunday will consist of an envelope-stuffing party.
key_insight: Grassroots financial education efforts like Occupy Finance can attract mainstream media attention even with DIY production mishaps.
